page.content: The table presents a comprehensive overview of various biological databases relevant to transcription factors and regulatory elements, including their descriptions and URLs. The first entry, ACTIVITY, is a Functional DNA/RNA site activity database accessible at http://wwwmgs.bionet.nsc.ru/mgs/systems/activity/. The second entry, DoOP, is the Database of orthologous promoters focused on chordates and plants, found at http://doop.abc.hu/. The EPD, or Eukaryotic promoter database, is detailed at http://www.epd.isb-sib.ch/. JASPAR offers PSSMs for transcription factor DNA-binding sites and can be accessed via http://jaspar.cgb.ki.se/. MAPPER provides information on putative transcription factor binding sites in various genomes, with its URL being http://bio.chip.org/mapper.

page.content: The TESS database, or Transcription Element Search System, is available at http://www.cbil.upenn.edu/tess/. TRANSCompel contains composite regulatory elements affecting gene transcription in eukaryotes and is found at http://www.gene-regulation.com/pub/databases.html#transcompel. TRED, the Transcriptional Regulatory Element Database, can be accessed at http://rulai.cshl.edu/tred/. The TRRD, or Transcription Regulatory Regions of Eukaryotic Genes, is located at http://www.bionet.nsc.ru/trrd/. AthaMap offers a genome-wide map of putative transcription factor binding sites in Arabidopsis thaliana, accessible at http://www.athamap.de/. Lastly, the DATF, or Database of Arabidopsis Transcription Factors, is found at http://datf.cbi.pku.edu.cn/. Each database provides valuable resources for researchers in the field of genomics and transcriptional regulation.
